query: Process this whole CSV of findings — lists/headct_findings.csv — and create stubs for anything not already in the library.
I have a directory of markdown stubs describing 20 new knee MRI findings. Please create models for all of them and then we'll review.
Here's a list of 15 abdominal ultrasound findings. Please batch-create models with my usual source and contributors.
Can you batch-ingest the findings in /tmp/new_brain_mri_findings.csv? Row ID column is 'FID', OIFM column doesn't exist yet.
I need you to work through this list of pediatric chest radiograph findings and build out stubs for each one.
Bulk-create finding models for the 12 rows in this spreadsheet; after creation, I want to review them in the TUI before anything is finalized.
Take each file in ./draft_findings/ (markdown, one finding per file) and turn it into a proper fm.json model.
Go through headct_findings.csv, triage existing matches vs new, and create models for everything that doesn't exist yet.
I'm pasting 8 cardiac MRI findings below — please create models for all of them and write OIFM IDs back to the CSV.
Process this directory of Hood-format JSON finding descriptions and generate models. Wait — scratch the Hood format, here they are as markdown instead.
Do we already have a finding model for cerebral microbleeds? If not, please add one.
Add 'SDH' as a synonym to the subdural hematoma model.
Review all the fracture finding models in defs/ and flag anything whose change-from-prior values look wrong.
Audit defs/*_hemorrhage.fm.json for naming and synonym issues.
Check whether we have 'nephrolithiasis' as a finding model.
Can you fix the description on the existing subdural hematoma model?
Run the validator script on the defs/ directory and report any failures.
Walk through every finding model tagged 'brain' and tell me what needs improvement.
Explain how SNOMED index codes work in this project.
Write me a script to generate a summary of all models by organ system.
